recipes.title: Nasi Lemak
recipes.description: Malaysia's national dish of pandan-coconut rice with sambal, peanuts, anchovies, egg and cucumber.
steps: Rinse rice and cook with coconut milk, pandan, salt and water in a rice cooker.
Soak dried chillies in hot water, then blend with shallots, garlic and belacan into a paste.
Saute paste in oil over low heat for 20 minutes until very fragrant and oil separates.
Add tamarind, palm sugar and a splash of water; simmer 10 minutes to make sambal.
Fry anchovies and peanuts separately in oil until crisp.
Hard-boil eggs and slice cucumber.
Mound rice on plates and arrange sambal, anchovies, peanuts, egg and cucumber around it.

recipes.title: Laksa
recipes.description: Spicy coconut noodle soup with shrimp, fish cake, tofu puffs, bean sprouts and laksa leaves.
steps: Soak dried chillies and blend with shallots, garlic, galangal, lemongrass, belacan and candlenuts.
Saute the spice paste in oil for 15 minutes until red and fragrant.
Add shrimp stock and simmer 10 minutes, then add coconut milk.
Add fish cake and tofu puffs and simmer until heated through.
Add shrimp and cook just until pink.
Cook noodles separately and divide between bowls with bean sprouts.
Ladle hot laksa over noodles, garnish with laksa leaves and lime.

recipes.title: Char Kway Teow
recipes.description: Malaysian wok-fried flat rice noodles with prawns, cockles, Chinese sausage, egg and bean sprouts.
steps: Heat a wok smoking hot and melt lard.
Stir-fry chopped garlic and chilli paste briefly, then add Chinese sausage and shrimp.
Push to the side and crack in eggs, scrambling lightly.
Add noodles and toss with dark and light soy sauce, distributing color evenly.
Add cockles, bean sprouts and garlic chives, toss for 30 seconds for wok hei.
Season with white pepper and serve immediately on a hot plate.

recipes.title: Roti Canai
recipes.description: Malaysian flaky flatbread, slapped and stretched thin then pan-cooked, served with curry dipping sauce.
steps: Mix flour, salt, sugar, condensed milk, egg and water into a soft dough; knead 10 minutes.
Divide into balls, coat generously in oil and rest in a covered tray for 4 hours.
Saute onion and curry powder, add diced potato and coconut milk; simmer until potato is tender.
Oil a clean surface and slap a dough ball flat, then stretch tissue-thin with quick flicks.
Brush with ghee, fold into a square and pan-cook on a hot oiled griddle until golden and flaky.
Crush each cooked roti slightly to fluff the layers and serve with curry dipping sauce.

recipes.title: Satay
recipes.description: Malaysian skewers of marinated meat grilled over charcoal, served with peanut sauce and rice cake.
steps: Toast coriander and cumin seeds, then grind with shallots, garlic, galangal, turmeric and lemongrass.
Mix half the paste with sugar and salt, marinate chicken cubes for 4 hours.
Soak bamboo skewers in water for 30 minutes.
For sauce, fry the remaining paste, add ground roasted peanuts, tamarind, coconut milk and water; simmer until thick.
Thread chicken onto skewers, packing pieces close together.
Grill over hot charcoal for 8 minutes, brushing with oil and turning often.
Serve skewers with peanut sauce, ketupat rice cakes, cucumber and onion.

recipes.title: Curry Laksa
recipes.description: Coconut curry noodle soup with chicken, tofu puffs, prawns and bean sprouts, also called curry mee.
steps: Soak dried chillies, then blend with shallots, garlic, lemongrass and belacan.
Fry the paste in oil with curry powder until aromatic and red.
Add chicken stock and simmer chicken thighs until cooked, then shred.
Stir in coconut milk and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add tofu puffs and shrimp; cook until shrimp turns pink.
Cook noodles separately and divide between bowls with bean sprouts.
Top with shredded chicken and shrimp, ladle hot curry broth over and serve with lime.

recipes.title: Hainanese Chicken Rice
recipes.description: Poached chicken served with fragrant chicken-fat rice, ginger-scallion sauce and chilli-garlic dip.
steps: Stuff chicken cavity with smashed ginger, scallions and garlic, then rub with salt.
Bring a large pot of water to boil, dunk and lift chicken three times, then submerge and simmer 35 minutes.
Plunge chicken into ice water, then rub with sesame oil and reserve poaching stock.
Saute minced ginger and garlic in oil, add rinsed rice and toast 1 minute.
Add poaching stock and pandan, cook covered until rice is fluffy and fragrant.
Blend chillies, garlic, ginger, lime and a little stock for chilli sauce; mix grated ginger with hot oil for ginger sauce.
Chop chicken into pieces, plate with cucumber and cilantro, and serve with rice, soy and dipping sauces.

recipes.title: Beef Rendang
recipes.description: Malay-style slow-cooked beef in coconut milk, kerisik and spice paste until dry and dark.
steps: Soak dried chillies, then blend with shallots, garlic, galangal, ginger, turmeric and lemongrass.
Cook the paste with cinnamon and star anise in oil until very fragrant.
Add cubed beef and coat in spices.
Pour in coconut milk, lime leaves and turmeric leaves; simmer uncovered.
Cook 2.5 hours, stirring more often as it dries down.
Stir in kerisik, tamarind and salt, cook 20 more minutes until very dark and oily.
Serve with steamed rice or nasi lemak.

recipes.title: Mee Goreng
recipes.description: Mamak-style stir-fried yellow noodles with potato, prawns, tofu and a sweet-spicy tomato chilli sauce.
steps: Boil and dice the potato until just tender; cube and fry tofu until golden.
Heat oil and fry chopped shallots and garlic until fragrant.
Add shrimp and cook 1 minute.
Push aside and crack eggs in, scramble lightly.
Add noodles, potato, tofu, mustard greens and bean sprouts.
Stir in tomato sauce, chilli sauce, soy and kecap manis; toss until well coated.
Serve immediately with lime wedges.

recipes.title: Asam Pedas
recipes.description: Malay sour-spicy fish stew with tamarind, chilli paste, lady's fingers and torch ginger flower.
steps: Soak dried chillies, then blend with shallots, garlic, belacan, galangal and turmeric.
Saute paste in oil with smashed lemongrass for 10 minutes until red.
Mix tamarind with water and pour into the pot.
Add tomato wedges, sliced torch ginger flower, sugar and salt; simmer 10 minutes.
Add fish steaks and lady's fingers, simmer gently 10 minutes until fish is cooked.
Stir in Vietnamese mint and serve with steamed rice.

recipes.title: Cendol
recipes.description: Malaysian iced dessert of pandan jelly noodles, red beans, palm sugar syrup and coconut milk.
steps: Whisk rice flour, tapioca, pandan juice, water and salt in a saucepan.
Cook over medium heat, stirring until very thick and translucent.
Press the hot batter through a cendol mould into a bowl of ice water; the strands will set.
Drain cendol noodles.
Simmer palm sugar with pandan leaves and a splash of water until syrupy, then cool.
Warm coconut milk with a pinch of salt, then chill.
In tall glasses, layer cendol noodles, red beans and shaved ice; pour over coconut milk and palm sugar syrup.

recipes.title: Nasi Kerabu
recipes.description: Kelantan blue rice with shredded herbs, fried fish, salted egg, coconut crumb and budu sauce.
steps: Steep butterfly pea flowers in hot water and use the blue liquid to cook the rice.
Marinate mackerel with turmeric and salt, then deep-fry until crisp.
Toast grated coconut with pounded chillies, galangal and lemongrass until dark and fragrant for serunding.
Finely shred long beans, cabbage, mint and Vietnamese mint with thinly sliced torch ginger flower.
Mix budu with lime and chopped chillies for the dressing.
Mound blue rice on plates and arrange shredded herbs, salted egg, fried fish and serunding around it.
Pour budu dressing over before eating.
